records.title: Deployable compact optical ground station as a commercial access model

structured_summary.core_concept: Archangel Lightworks' TERRA-M is reported as a compact deployable optical ground station for secure, rapid LEO links, with a physical envelope of about 1.1 m height and 0.7 m diameter.
structured_summary.gs_linkops_relevance: Introduces a mobile optical-station asset class that can be booked, purchased or deployed temporarily rather than treated as a permanently installed observatory.
structured_summary.reported_validation: A June 2026 report states that TERRA-M completed multi-day tests against the U.S. Space Development Agency laser-communication standard and that purchase and service-contract options were available; these commercial and conformance claims require primary-source confirmation.

pending_confirmation: Exact SDA standard profile and test report
Pricing, lead time and service territory

sources.title: World's smallest space laser station is changing global connectivity

records.title: OptiSat cloud-aware onboard processing and optical ground-station selection

structured_summary.core_concept: OptiSat is a 6U Greek technology-demonstration CubeSat launched on 30 March 2026 with a TESAT SCOT20 optical terminal and onboard processing intended to support optical-link decisions, including selection of cloud-free ground stations.
structured_summary.gs_linkops_relevance: Adds a satellite-side decision layer where ground-station choice is influenced by onboard cloud screening and optical-link suitability rather than only by precomputed visibility windows.
structured_summary.operational_implication: The scheduling engine should accept spacecraft-generated station preference, cloud-avoidance flags and product-priority decisions as dynamic inputs.

pending_confirmation: Official mission operations status after launch
SCOT20 wavelength, line rate and protocol profile

records.title: Abu Dhabi quantum optical ground station readiness and link-performance evidence

structured_summary.core_concept: A terrestrial readiness campaign used the engineering model of the SpeQtre satellite entangled-photon payload with the Abu Dhabi Quantum Optical Ground Station over a 1.8 km free-space link.
structured_summary.gs_linkops_relevance: Provides an evidence model for recording pre-flight ground-segment compatibility tests, including protocol, test distance, secret-key rate, quantum-bit-error rate and atmospheric background-rejection configuration.
structured_summary.reported_result: The paper reports a BBM92 secret-key rate of approximately 7.56 kbps and a mean QBER of 4.78% ± 0.24% under the stated terrestrial test conditions; these values are test evidence, not an orbital service SLA.

sources.title: Terrestrial readiness campaign for space-to-ground quantum communications with a space-qualified entangled photon-pair system
